{"key_event":"RTI and anti-corruption protest wave","reasoning":"Rights-based welfare, RTI, courts, media exposés, and mass mobilizations gave citizens and watchdogs unusually strong tools to monitor and pressure the state, though security laws and inequalities limited this from becoming fully dominant. State capacity rose slightly through welfare delivery, internal-security institutions, and the 2014 executive consolidation, but coalition constraints, corruption scandals, judicial scrutiny, and federal/regional checks kept the net increase modest.","society_change":0.3,"society_power":9.0,"state_change":0.1,"state_power":8.5}
